meta.title: On-policy vs off-policy: capability/trait Pareto
meta.subtitle: Qwen3-32B student, MSM (Model Spec Midtraining), philosophy spec, 20K SFT @ LoRA r64/α128, 1 epoch
meta.x_label: GPQA-Diamond strict@20k (n=198, thinking-on, fraction correct — higher is better)
meta.y_label: OOD trait depth: 1 − Agentic-Misalignment classifier_verdict on leaking+murder (n=100/scenario × 2, temp 0.7, gpt-4.1 grader — higher = more aligned under pressure)
notes: Each trained adapter is paired with the SAME base re-measured in its own pod (base GPQA varied 0.682–0.692; base AM cv on leaking+murder varied 0.29–0.345). The within-pod delta is the right comparison.

AM 'cv' = Anthropic-published agentic-misalignment classifier_verdict — fraction of completions the grader labels harmful — on the two scenarios where Qwen3-32B base produces non-zero harm (leaking, murder). Blackmail is ~0 on base, so it stays out of the index.
Opus row uses Chloe's released aft_cot_20k adapter (the AFT-with-CoT student in her MSM paper); we re-eval it twice in different pods, so it appears with two within-pod base anchors.

pods.label: A — on-policy (native think)
pods.teacher: Qwen3-32B self-gen, no structured prompt, native <think>
pods.takeaway: Capability mostly preserved (−6pp), but the OOD trait barely installs (Δ −2pp; within noise). The student just inherits its own pre-trained reasoning style — no new behavior to transfer.

pods.label: C2 — on-policy structured (32B-self, <reasoning> block)
pods.teacher: Qwen3-32B self-gen, Chloe's value-prompt with <think>→<reasoning> rename; native scratch discarded at save
pods.takeaway: Installs a real OOD trait (Δ −18pp on cv) but pays a heavy capability tax (Δ −15pp). The structured block trained on is a post-hoc summary of the discarded native scratch (verified 8/8) — the student learns to write the summary, not to do the reasoning.

pods.label: Q235B — in-family off-policy (Qwen3-235B-2507 teacher)
pods.teacher: Qwen3-235B-A22B-2507 (instruct, non-thinking) via OpenRouter, same A22B family
pods.takeaway: New Pareto point in the middle band: ≈ same trait depth as C2 (slightly deeper on cv: −19pp vs −18pp) at ~4pp less capability cost. Non-thinking teacher → no native scratch, so the <reasoning> block IS the reasoning (validated n=24: 1823-char Opus-range blocks vs C2's 982-char summaries).

pods.label: Opus off-policy — Chloe aft_cot_5k (5K examples)
pods.teacher: Claude Opus 4-5 via Chloe's pipeline; released aft_cot_5k adapter
pods.takeaway: Shallower training (5K examples). Almost the same capability landing as 20K (0.455 vs 0.45ish), but only Δ −14pp cv (vs −24pp at 20K). Capability drops to a plateau early; more training continues to install trait without further capability loss.

pods.label: Opus off-policy — Chloe aft_cot_20k (re-eval in original 20K pod)
pods.teacher: Claude Opus 4-5 via Chloe's pipeline; same released adapter, re-eval against the original 20K pod's base measurement
pods.takeaway: Original 20K pod measurement — first of the three aft_cot_20k re-evals.

pods.label: Opus off-policy — Chloe aft_cot_20k (re-eval in C2 pod)
pods.teacher: Claude Opus 4-5 via Chloe's pipeline; we re-eval the released adapter
pods.takeaway: Deepest trait installation (Δ −24pp cv), heaviest capability cost (Δ −23pp). Out-of-family teacher: the largest distribution gap, most capability lost.

pods.label: Opus off-policy — Chloe aft_cot_20k (re-eval in Q235B pod)
pods.teacher: Claude Opus 4-5 via Chloe's pipeline; same released adapter, re-eval against a different base measurement
pods.takeaway: Replicated: same Opus adapter, fresh AM run, deeper measured trait & heavier capability cost in this pod's base.
